summary: Prove the authoritative R1 runtime through hermetic production-path end-to-end evidence
acceptance_criteria: Playwright starts the normal production Web entry without fixture injection, fake transport, Storybook-only state, canary substitution, or test-only in-process simulation and proves a real Worker boot.

The browser scenario reads the initial model, previews a real cost, submits a legal command, observes Worker acceptance and a changed authoritative state hash, then observes its revisioned read-model delta.
Invalid and expired commands produce stable rejection reasons, unchanged authoritative hashes, and rejected UI state.
The scenario exercises pause, 1x, 2x, 4x, deterministic auto-pause, and at least one complete in-game month with scheduled settlement.
The scenario saves, closes the browser/runtime, starts a fresh Worker, loads the save, and proves identical hash, calendar, scheduler tail, and visible read model.
A forced Worker crash proves reconnect and recovery behavior without silent fixture boot or no-op success.
The same seed, content, command log, save, and replay tail produce matching browser Worker and Node hashes with artifacted command, revision, lifecycle, and save evidence.
Evidence distinguishes normal Web, Electron security/save smoke, Node runner, Storybook, Playwright, and canary categories; test/demo evidence is never relabeled as production behavior.
required_tests: artifact assertion proving a real Worker URL/session, accepted and rejected commands, state hashes, revisions, one-month advance, save/restart/load, crash recovery, and browser/Node parity
notes: This task may add hermetic harnesses and evidence only; it must route product defects back to the owning implementation task rather than patch product code.
No test may pass by detecting test mode and replacing the production Worker path.
